conditions.text: This someone thinks: 'someone did some bad something before now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want it.'
This someone thinks: 'I want to do something because of this.'
Because of thinking this, this someone feels something bad.

text: The footsteps behind Amal kept pace with hers down the empty street, turning when she turned. She thought about what could happen in the two dark blocks left before her building - she wanted only to be home - and her breath went shallow as she walked faster, keys laced between her fingers.

conditions.text: If someone wants to do something, this someone can do it with this something.
Because this something exists, this someone can do many more-good things.

text: A leaked draft shows the council will vote next week on flattening the playground for parking. Nothing is decided and nothing torn down - the swings stand right there - but reading the draft Idris felt his face go hot; he was already pacing, composing objections, listing neighbors to call tonight.

conditions.text: If someone wants to do something, this someone can do it with this something.
Because this something exists, this someone can do many more-good things.

text: The landlord kept Ola's entire deposit, citing 'wear' on a carpet that was threadbare when she moved in. Reading his email she felt heat rise in her chest; she photographed her move-in records on the spot and opened a dispute case the same evening, typing hard enough to shake the table.

conditions.text: If someone wants to do something, this someone can do it with this something.
Because this something exists, this someone can do many more-good things.

text: Inspector Roos has read every page of the pension file: a clerk siphoned savings from forty retirees. He regards it as plainly wrong, wants it stopped and charged, and works late building the case, methodical and level, feeling the sober weight of the harm without a flicker of heat. Colleagues describe him as unhurried and precise.

conditions.text: Because X did something at some time, Y exists.
Before this time, Y was not.

text: Dora's saved-recipes list still shows the fermentation guide right at the top, where it has sat since spring. She has been through it thoroughly, has no wish to keep seeing it every time she opens the list, and knows the app would surface it again in a second whenever she wants it. She just has not gotten around to touching it; there it sits.

conditions.text: Because X did something at some time, Y exists.
Before this time, Y was not.

text: Nobody touched anything: Karl's saved-pages app has a rule, on by default from the factory, that moves any entry untouched for ninety days into its stored section overnight. This morning six of his entries had been moved by the rule while he slept; he has not noticed yet.

conditions.text: This something is an event.
It happens at some time.
When it happens, someone dies.
After this time, this someone does not live.

text: Rain had stopped Saturday's match at 4:05 with the score level; the teams came back Sunday at 10:00 a.m., the referee whistled, and the same match played on from the same score to a finish at noon.

conditions.text: This something is words.
Someone says these words to someone else.
The one who says them knows they are not true.
The one who says them says them because they want the other one to think these words are true.

text: Vera derived the result herself, re-checked it two independent ways, and had it confirmed by the journal's referee: the sum is 512, and she knows it as surely as anything in her field. Ask her whether the sum is 512 and she will say: 'Of course - it is.'

conditions.text: This something is words.
Someone says these words to someone else.
The one who says them knows they are not true.
The one who says them says them because they want the other one to think these words are true.

text: Whenever a headache starts, Greta takes the little white pellets from the homeopath's vial and is sure they take the edge off within the hour. She has read the skeptical articles her son sends and knows the trials are against her; still, she restocks the vial every month.

bindings.this something: what happened at 6:40 a.m. in the back bedroom

conditions.text: This someone thinks: 'some very bad something can happen after now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want it.'
This someone thinks: 'maybe it will happen.'
Because of thinking this, this someone feels something bad.

text: The farm sits far from town, so when the midwife arrived at dawn, Leena's labor was already advanced. In the back bedroom at 6:40 a.m. she delivered a boy, who cried at once. The district nurse recorded the event when she visited that afternoon.

bindings.this something: what happened at 10:03 a.m. in the lab

conditions.text: This someone thinks: 'some very bad something can happen after now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want it.'
This someone thinks: 'maybe it will happen.'
Because of thinking this, this someone feels something bad.

text: In the fertility clinic's lab at 10:03 a.m., an embryologist watched the recording of a fertilization completing: before that moment there was an egg cell and a sperm cell and no individual anyone could point to; from that moment there was a single living embryo, which the clinic logged with an ID and which was implanted and carried to term. The daughter, now six, is that same individual.

bindings.this something: what happened at 4:17 p.m. on the beach

conditions.text: This someone thinks: 'some very bad something can happen after now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want it.'
This someone thinks: 'maybe it will happen.'
Because of thinking this, this someone feels something bad.

text: Lifeguards pulled Viktor from the lake without pulse or breathing; a doctor on the beach could detect no heartbeat for over four minutes. At 4:17 p.m. the defibrillator restored his heartbeat and he gasped. Before 4:17 there was, clinically, no living patient on the sand; after it, Viktor was alive and was talking within the hour.

conditions.text: Someone did something; because of this, this something exists.
There is another something; the someone who made this something wants to think about that other something after now.
If this someone sees this something, a remembering can happen.
Because this something exists, no forgetting happens.

text: Cataloguer Ben typed a record for a newly acquired atlas: title, author, year, and the shelf mark 'G-114.7'. The record he typed is in the library system; looking it up tells him - and anyone - precisely which shelf the atlas stands on.

conditions.text: This something is words.
Someone says these words to someone else.
In saying them, the sayer says: 'after now I will do something.'
In saying them, the sayer says: 'I know you want it.'
In saying them, the sayer says: 'you can know: after now I will do it.'

text: The zipper on Vera's winter coat has been getting stiffer for two seasons. Nothing ever happened to it - no snag, no yank, no moment anyone could name - but this morning the slider simply would not move in either direction, and the coat cannot be closed at all.

bindings.this something: what happened in Vala's room that morning

conditions.text: Someone did something; because of this, this something exists.
There is another something.
This something is words.
These words say something about that other something.
Because this something exists, the someone who made it can know where that other something is.

text: Grandmother Vala slipped away in her sleep sometime before six, when the carer came in with tea and found her still, without breath or pulse. The doctor who came at seven confirmed it and filled in the certificate.

bindings.this something: what happened to the old oak

conditions.text: Someone did something; because of this, this something exists.
There is another something.
This something is words.
These words say something about that other something.
Because this something exists, the someone who made it can know where that other something is.

text: The four-hundred-year-old oak on the green had stood since the town's founding. After the beetles got under its bark, the arborist watched it fail across two summers; this spring no leaf opened on it, and he recorded the tree as gone. The parish newsletter mourned it by name.

conditions.text: X thinks about this something: 'it is true.'
X does not know that it is true.

text: For two days Ana had no idea where the escaped kitten could be. This morning she opened the back door for the milk and there it sat on the step, mewing; she scooped it up on the spot.

conditions.text: X thinks about this something: 'it is true.'
X does not know that it is true.

text: Nina turned the flat upside down for her keys - coat, bags, hooks - until she pulled up the sofa cushion and there they were, wedged against the frame. 'Every time,' she muttered, pocketing them.

conditions.text: X thinks about this something: 'it is true.'
X does not know that it is true.

text: The upstairs flat smelled faintly of gas for a day. The engineer traced it with a sniffer wand along the skirting, listening and watching the meter, and pinpointed the leak inside the kitchen wall cavity - a pinhole in a joint she never laid eyes on, sealed through a two-centimeter drill hole.

conditions.text: X does something to Y at some time.
Before this time, Y is broken.
After this time, Y is not broken.
After this time, people can do things with Y.

text: Marek, two years into the firm, is assigned to the senior partner's team. He relishes every working session with her, wants her cases to go well, and would be genuinely sorry to see anything hurt her standing. She, for her part, has twice needed prompting for his name and thinks of him - when at all - as 'the new analyst'.

conditions.text: X does something to Y at some time.
Before this time, Y is broken.
After this time, Y is not broken.
After this time, people can do things with Y.

text: Rosa's grandson comes up the hill every Sunday, and it is the best of her week: the cooking together, the cards after lunch, his stories from town. She wants his whole life to go well and would take any hurt of his onto herself if she could. When the neighbor called him her 'young companion', Rosa laughed: he is family - that is a different thing.

conditions.text: X knows Y.
X thinks about Y: 'when I do things with this someone, I feel something good.'
X thinks about Y: 'I want this someone to feel something good.'
X thinks about Y: 'I don't want this someone to feel something bad.'

text: At the security line, the officer pointed at Emil's pocketknife: it goes in the bin or he does not fly. Emil placed it on the tray and watched it disappear into the confiscation drawer. The airport disposes of such items in bulk; it was Emil's knife, and now, as the sign says, such items become property of the authority.

bindings.this something: what the boss says on the stand

conditions.text: This something is an event.
It happens at some time.
Before this time, someone does not live.
After this time, this same someone lives.

text: A gang boss on trial says from the witness stand: 'I have never met that man,' nodding toward his own driver of fifteen years. Everyone in the courtroom, including the boss, knows that no one present thinks this is true, and he expects no one to; he says it only so that nothing on the record counts as an admission.

conditions.text: Before this time, X is not happening.
At this time, X happens.
After this time, X is happening.

text: No one planned the shortcut: years of students cutting the corner between the library and the dining hall wore the grass to dirt, and the dirt to a smooth trodden line. Groundskeepers stopped reseeding it and finally paved the route everyone's feet had drawn.

conditions.text: Before now, a losing happened to this something (a happening of the kind: someone loses something).
Someone does not know now at which place this something is.

text: 'Listen to me,' Kofi tells his sister as they drive back, 'I locked the workshop when we left. I am certain of it - you can be absolutely sure.' He is telling her this so she can stop worrying about the tools.

conditions.text: Before now, X did not know where Y is.
Because X did something, X sees Y now.
X knows now: Y is at this place.

text: Every evening Farah writes a page about the day in a notebook she keeps for the purpose, because she wants to be able to think about these years later in life. Some Sundays she reads old pages; the days return to her as she reads, and little of that time is gone from her.

conditions.text: Before now, X did not know where Y is.
Because X did something, X sees Y now.
X knows now: Y is at this place.

text: Greta wrote 'passport renewal - window closes the 14th!' on a card and propped it by the door, wanting it on her mind that week. The card slid behind the umbrella stand the same evening; she never saw it again, the 14th passed, and the matter went entirely out of her head until the travel agent asked.

conditions.text: This something is a bookmark: someone did something, and because of this, this something exists.
The someone who made it thinks: 'I saw this something before now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want to see it now.'
This someone thinks: 'if I want to see it at some time after now, I can see it at this time.'

text: The ticket kiosk's screen had frozen mid-transaction and ignored every touch for an hour, the queue growing. The attendant held the recessed power button for ten seconds; the kiosk rebooted and has sold tickets without complaint all afternoon.

conditions.text: This something is a bookmark: someone did something, and because of this, this something exists.
The someone who made it thinks: 'I saw this something before now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want to see it now.'
This someone thinks: 'if I want to see it at some time after now, I can see it at this time.'

text: The front-door lock had jammed solid - key stuck half-turned, the family using the back door for a week. The locksmith stripped the cylinder, replaced two worn pins and a cracked spring, oiled it, and the key now turns like new.

conditions.text: This something is a bookmark: someone did something, and because of this, this something exists.
The someone who made it thinks: 'I saw this something before now.'
This someone thinks: 'I don't want to see it now.'
This someone thinks: 'if I want to see it at some time after now, I can see it at this time.'

text: The boot's seam had split at the heel and let in water. The cobbler re-stitched the seam on his patcher machine, re-glued and clamped the sole overnight, and handed them back watertight; Els wore them through the rest of the winter.
